steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B (19)
steps.content:
If there are 19 two-person tents, then the number of four-person tents is $$56 - 19 = 37$$.
Total people would be $$2(19) + 4(37) = 38 + 148 = 186$$.
Since 186 is not equal to 188, choice B (‘19’) fails.

steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice C (18)
steps.content:
If there are 18 two-person tents, then the number of four-person tents is $$56 - 18 = 38$$.
Total people would be $$2(18) + 4(38) = 36 + 152 = 188$$.
That confirms C is correct.
